peter-the-great.name: Peter the Great
peter-the-great.description: Drags Russia into modern times by its beard. 6'8" tall and carries scissors.

catherine-the-great.name: Catherine the Great
catherine-the-great.description: German princess who became the greatest Russian Empress. Friend of Voltaire.

rasputin.name: Grigori Rasputin
rasputin.description: Mystic healer who refused to die. Brought down an empire.

constitutional-russia.title: Constitutional Monarchy 📜👑
constitutional-russia.description: The Decembrist Revolt succeeds. Russia becomes a democracy in 1825.
constitutional-russia.divergence_description: The army supports the Constitution instead of Nicholas I.
constitutional-russia.present_day_status: The Russian Federation is a constitutional monarchy similar to the UK. St. Petersburg is the "Paris of the North". 🇷🇺👑

constitution-1825.title: The Constitution
constitution-1825.description: Tsar Constantine signs the Constitution. Serfdom is abolished early.

traditionalist-russia.title: Old Muscovy 🐻⛪
traditionalist-russia.description: Peter reforms fail. Russia remains an isolated, medieval state.
traditionalist-russia.present_day_status: The Principality of Moscow is a small, quiet nation known for its churches and fur trade. The Swedish Empire controls the Baltic. 🇸🇪🏰

isolation.title: The Great Wall of Russia
isolation.description: Russia closes its borders to the corrupted West.

partition.long_term: Russia is partitioned by Sweden and Turkey

sino-russian.title: The Dragon and Bear 🐉🐻
sino-russian.description: Russia turns East instead of West.
sino-russian.present_day_status: The Eurasian Union is the world's superpower. The Silk Road is a high-speed rail network. 🌏🚂

eurasian-pact.title: Eurasian Pact
eurasian-pact.description: A military alliance that dominates Asia.
eurasian-pact.impact: End of Western colonialism in Asia.

beard-tax.title: The Beard Tax (1698)
beard-tax.text: Moscow. Tsar Peter has returned from Europe. He holds a pair of scissors. "The beard is a useless burden," he says. "Pay the tax or shave!"

shave.consequence: You lose your dignity but gain the Tsar's favor. Russia modernizes.

rebel.consequence: The Streltsy rebel. Peter is overthrown. Russia stays medieval.

decembrist-revolt.title: Senate Square (1825)
decembrist-revolt.text: Alexander I is dead. The army is in the square. They shout "Constantine and Constitution!" Nicholas I orders the cannons loaded.

crush.text: Fire the cannons! Order must be maintained.
crush.consequence: The revolt is crushed. Autocracy survives.

join.text: Join the rebels! For the Constitution!
join.consequence: The army mutinies. Nicholas flees. A Republic is born.

emancipation.title: The Serf Question (1861)
emancipation.text: Tsar Alexander II asks your advice. "It is better to abolish serfdom from above than have it abolished from below." But the nobles are angry.

land.text: Give the peasants land. Full freedom.
land.consequence: The nobles are furious, but the peasants prosper. Revolution is averted.

east.text: Forget Europe. Look East to China.
east.consequence: We forge a new destiny in Asia.

rasputin.title: The Mad Monk (1916)
rasputin.text: Rasputin is throwing a party. The Tsarina listens only to him. Russia is losing the war. Prince Yusupov offers you a poisoned cake.

kill.text: Help kill him. Save the reputation of the throne.
kill.consequence: It is too late. The revolution comes anyway.

save.text: Warn him. He is the only one who opposes the war.
save.consequence: Rasputin convinces the Tsar to make peace. The Empire survives (barely).
